operating_state: Total revenue increased 20.1% to $2.3 billion driven by Telxius acquisition and broad-based property revenue growth across all regions. Property revenue reached $2.2 billion with straight-line revenue contributing materially, while services revenue expanded significantly. Six-month revenue grew 14.1% to $4.5 billion with colocations, amendments, and contractual escalations contributing net of churn.
cost_structure: Property operating costs increased primarily due to acquired operations while segment gross margin expanded to $1.65 billion maintaining strong margins despite acquisition-related costs. Depreciation and amortization expanded driven by intangible asset amortization from acquisitions. Capital expenditures totaled $620.2 million for six months with full-year expectations of $1.5-1.6 billion including construction of communications sites globally.
material_events: Completed $8.9 billion Telxius acquisition in June 2021 acquiring approximately 27,000 communications sites with remaining German sites expected to close in August 2021. Raised $2.4 billion through common stock offering and issued senior notes across multiple tranches. Entered agreements with CDPQ and Allianz for them to acquire noncontrolling interests in ATC Europe for aggregate consideration of approximately €2.6 billion while establishing new credit facilities totaling $7.0 billion.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in $118.8 million positive impact for the quarter but negative impact for the six-month period, with significant revenues and operating expenses denominated in foreign currencies creating potential unrealized losses from adverse exchange rate movements. Interest rate exposure increased through variable-rate debt facilities requiring LIBOR transition by December 2022.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued revenue growth from newly acquired European and Latin American assets with integration synergies materializing over time and substantial non-cancellable tenant lease revenue over future periods. Capital allocation focused on debt reduction using operating cash flows while maintaining investment in network densification opportunities. Leverage covenant temporarily increased to 7.5x EBITDA for four quarters post-Telxius closing, stepping down thereafter.
uncertainty_profile: Integration risks from the large-scale Telxius acquisition including operational complexity and currency exposure management across multiple jurisdictions. Outstanding tax assessments include unrecognized tax benefits and claims with potential changes over the next 12 months. Asset retirement obligations increased reflecting expanded site portfolio and evolving environmental requirements. CDPQ and Allianz partnership transactions remain subject to regulatory approvals.
